data.text/markdown:
SO-CPR Data Preprocessing Pipeline

This notebook preprocesses the Southern Ocean Continuous Plankton Recorder (SO-CPR) dataset. It translates the original R pipeline into Python, covering:
  1. Load & inspect the raw CPR data
  2. Parse taxon names from species columns
  3. Annotate taxonomy via WoRMS (World Register of Marine Species)
  4. Calculate indices (total abundance, Salp/Euphausiid balance)
1. Load & Inspect Data

Dataset overview:
  • Rows (segments): 56,300
  • Total columns: 312
  • Metadata columns: 18
  • Species/taxon columns: 294
  • Year range: 1991 – 2023

data.text/markdown:
2. Parse Taxon Names

Each species column name encodes genus, species, life stage, and/or qualifier information. We parse these to prepare for WoRMS taxonomy lookup.

data.text/markdown:
3. WoRMS Taxonomy Annotation

Query the World Register of Marine Species (WoRMS) to build a full taxonomic hierarchy for each taxon. Results are cached to data/taxon_annotations.csv to avoid repeated API calls.

data.text/markdown:
4. Calculate Indices

  • Total abundance corrected by segment length
  • Salp / Euphausiid dominance index (positive = salp dominated)
5. Export Results

Saved outputs:
  • data/abundance_processed.csv (56,300 segments × 294 taxa)
  • data/salp_euphausiid_index.csv (56,300 segments)
  • data/metadata/segment_metadata.csv (56,300 segments — coordinates, env. variables, effort)
  • data/metadata/taxon_annotations.csv (292 taxa — WoRMS taxonomy)
